steps.n: This is a helper word that changes the verb after it (يَكُنِ) to mean the opposite in the past - like adding 'not' to 'was' to make 'was not'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show present tense
After لَمْ (not), the verb changes form - the و is dropped and the ending changes to show this is a negative past statement

steps.m: he be (after لَمْ, connected to next word)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to connect smoothly to the next word ٱلَّذِينَ
This is a relative pronoun used to describe a group of people
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The وا at the end shows that many people (they) did this action in the past. This word is the subject of لَمْ يَكُنِ (were not) in this ayah.

This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or what group something belongs to. In this ayah, it connects 'the people' (أَهْلِ) to 'those who disbelieved' (كَفَرُوا) to show which people are being talked about.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the noun form meaning 'people/family'
The kasra at the end shows this word owns or belongs to what comes after it. Here it connects with الكتاب (the Book) to mean 'people of the Book' (referring to Jews and Christians)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun representing the result of writing
Makes the word specific by adding 'the' at the beginning

steps.m: of the book/scripture (possessed by أَهْلِ - people)
steps.n: The kasra at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to the word أَهْلِ (people) before it, forming the phrase 'people of the book'
The م prefix turns the verb into a noun describing someone who does the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone who does this action
Added ين to make it plural (more than one)
Added ال to make it specific

steps.m: and the ones who associate partners
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at start and َ at end to connect it to the previous phrase
من at the start shows this is about someone affected by or doing the separation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make basic past tense verb
مُ at start shows this describes someone/something affected by the action
Added ينَ at the end to make it plural, showing it refers to multiple people
The word means 'those who separate' and the ين ending shows it's talking about multiple people
This is the basic form of the word that shows when something reaches its limit
Added the shadda to emphasize the meaning
Added the alif maqsura (ىٰ) at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it. In this ayah, it connects to تَأْتِيَهُمُ (comes to them)
Connected the root letters together
Added تَ at beginning to show 'she' and vowel marks to show present tense
Added هُمُ at the end to show who receives the action (them)
The doubled letter ي makes it stronger - something that's very clear
Doubling the middle root letter intensifies the basic meaning
Feminine ending transforms the adjective into a concrete noun
Adding definite article transforms the noun from indefinite to definite
The small diagonal mark above shows this word is doing the action (it's the doer/subject)
The و makes it mean 'someone who does this action a lot'
Added tanween to show this is indefinite (a messenger). This messenger is described in the ayah as being from Allah and reciting pure scriptures.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it shows that the messenger (رَسُولٌ) is from Allah (ٱللَّهِ).
The fatha at the end connects it to the next word (ٱللَّهِ)
The shadda adds emphasis to show the strength of the connection
Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and the hamza was dropped due to frequent use
Added shadda on ل to emphasize the letter, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added kasra at end to show this connects to the word مِّنَ (from) before it
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to sukun, and last vowel to damma with و to show ongoing action
Added ا at the end to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: to write on pages/scrolls, to spread out flat

steps.n: Added vowels to make the plural form that means multiple pages or written documents
Added ًا (tanween) to show these pages/scrolls are the direct object - they are being recited
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning more intense
Adding مُ at the start shows this describes something that received the purification
Adding ة makes this describe a feminine noun, like 'pages' which are feminine in Arabic
Adding ً (tanween) shows this is the object being described in the sentence, and the shadda on م shows it connects smoothly with the previous word
This is a basic preposition that shows location, containment or being inside something

steps.m: in it/within it (referring to the scrolls/writings mentioned after as كُتُبٌ)
steps.n: The ها suffix refers to the scrolls/writings (كُتُبٌ) mentioned right after this word in the ayah
Changes the verb into a noun meaning the result of writing - a book
Changes from one book to many books by changing the vowels inside the word (broken plural)
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject that قَيِّمَةٌ (upright/straight) describes
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
The middle letter و changes to ي and gets doubled (with shadda) to show intensity of the quality
Added ة to make the adjective feminine since it describes كُتُبٌ (books) which is feminine
Added tanween (double dammah) to show this is an indefinite noun
This is a basic particle that can mean different things based on context. Here it means 'not'.
Added و at the beginning to connect this to what came before, showing this is part of a sequence of ideas
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda shows the action was done with more force or repeatedly
Adding تَ at the beginning shows the action happened to the subject themselves. In this ayah, it refers to how those who were given the Book (الَّذِينَ) became divided.
Starting with the basic demonstrative pronoun and changing its form to connect with other parts
Adding ن at the end makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one

steps.m: those who (referring to the people who were given the Book)
steps.n: Adding ال with shadda at the beginning makes it point to specific people - in this case, the people who were given the Book (أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتٰبَ)
Changed to passive voice by adding ُ on first letter and ِ on middle letter. The ي at the end becomes َ.
The weak letter ي changes to و after the passive voice vowels
Added وا at the end to show that multiple people received the action. This refers back to الَّذِينَ (those who) in the ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
This pattern is often used for things that result from an action - a book is the result of writing. In this ayah, كِتَاب is what was given (object of أُوتُوا)
Adding the definite article ال makes the noun definite, changing it from 'a book' to 'the book'
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb أُوتُوا (were given)
Basic exception word that shows something is different from what was mentioned before

steps.m: except (excluding those who received the Book from being united)
steps.n: Added shadda and alif to strengthen and complete the exception meaning. In this ayah, it excludes the people who received the Book (ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتٰبَ) from being united.
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects with بَعْدِ (after) to mean 'from after'.
Added a small ن (called nun ghunna) at the end because it comes before the letter ب in بَعْدِ. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and form the basic noun meaning 'after'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being used after a preposition
This is a connecting word that links 'after' (بَعْدِ) with 'came to them' (جَآءَتْهُمُ) in this ayah. It helps explain what happened after something.
The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's a weak letter. Added fathas to show past tense.
Changed alif to madda (آ) for proper spelling of long vowel
Added ت at the end to show a female/feminine subject did the action
Added هُمُ to show the object (them) of the verb
Changed from a verb root meaning 'to make clear' into a noun meaning 'something clear' by adding vowels
Doubles middle letter to create intensive form
Adds feminine marker to create noun meaning 'clear evidence'
Added 'the' (ال) at the start and U sound (-ُ) at the end to make it specific and show it's doing the action
Added fatha to show this is being used as a negative particle that negates the verb أُمِرُوٓا۟ (they were commanded)
Added alif with madda (آ) to show elongated pronunciation. This is just a spelling variation and doesn't change the meaning.
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
This pattern makes the root into a noun meaning 'a single instance or occurrence'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to make it passive, showing the action was done to someone rather than by someone
Added وا with elongation to show multiple people were commanded. This word connects to لِيَعْبُدُوا (to worship) in the ayah, showing what they were commanded to do.
This is the basic form of the exception particle that means 'except' or 'only'. It connects to the previous statement 'they were not commanded' to show what is being excluded.
The middle letter is doubled to make the meaning stronger and clearer
The alif is added at the end to help connect this word to what comes after it. Here it connects to 'to worship' (لِيَعْبُدُوا۟) to show the only thing they were commanded to do.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added plural masculine ending to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: so that they may serve/worship (purpose of أُمِرُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Added لِ to show this is the purpose or goal of the command mentioned in أُمِرُوٓا۟ (they were commanded)
Added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form. This is a special spelling for this sacred name.

steps.m: Allah (as the object of لِيَعْبُدُوا - to worship)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being worshipped in the phrase 'to worship Allah'
Basic meaning of purity or sincerity
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added مُ prefix to show 'one who does' and changed inside vowels to make it mean 'the person who purifies'

steps.m: those who make (something) pure/sincere (describing the state of يَعْبُدُوا)
steps.n: Added ين ending to show this is plural and describes the state of those who worship (يَعْبُدُوا) in this ayah
Basic preposition showing direction or purpose
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for him/it (connected to يَعْبُدُوا - they worship)
steps.n: Added pronoun ه with damma to show who the preposition is directed to. This word connects to يَعْبُدُوا (they worship) to show who they are worshipping for.
Added kasra after first letter and made the root letters connect to form a noun meaning the system or way being followed
Added ال to make it specific/definite - now refers to THE way/system
Added fatha at end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: to turn away from wrong beliefs toward truth
steps.n: Basic meaning of turning away from wrong beliefs
Added vowels (َ ِ) to make the root into a past tense verb meaning 'he turned away from wrong beliefs'
Added ي to make it describe someone who does this action as their characteristic
Changed the word pattern to make it plural, showing many people who turn to truth

steps.m: those who turn toward truth (describing how they worship اللَّهَ)
steps.n: The alif is lengthened and a vowel mark added to show this word connects to 'Allah' - it tells us how they worship Him
The root meaning of standing/rising became associated with a group of people who stand together
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Added 'أ' at start to mean 'made someone/something do the action'
Changed to present tense with يُ prefix and internal vowel changes
Added conjunction prefix وَ to connect with previous phrase
Added plural suffix وا to show multiple people. This verb is connected to لِيَعْبُدُوا۟ (that they worship) in meaning and grammar.
The word becomes the noun 'prayer'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make them pronounceable
The و changes to ا and we add ة to make it a noun meaning one instance of prayer
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb يُقِيمُوا۟ (they establish)
The weak letter ي at the end affects how the word is formed
Connected the root letters together to form the basic word
Added يُ at the start to make it mean 'he gives' (present tense). The أ changed to ؤ because it comes after the ُ sound
Added وَ at beginning to connect with previous phrase about establishing prayer

steps.m: and they give (connected to لِيَعْبُدُوا۟ - so that they worship)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people doing the action. This matches with the earlier verb لِيَعْبُدُوا۟ (so that they worship). The small alif (۟) is just a pronunciation mark.
Basic meaning of purification or growth
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Added ة to make it into a noun representing one instance of the action
The ا makes it a noun emphasizing the act of purification
Added ال (the) at the start and changed the spelling slightly, making it mean the specific required charity in Islam
Changed the و to a long alif (ٰ) and added a fatha mark (َ) at the end - this is how we write the word for zakat (required charity) in the Quran
Starting with the basic demonstrative particle ذا and writing it with an alif above (ذٰ) which is a spelling variation
Adding لِ changes the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far)
ذٰلِ becomes ذٰلِكَ by adding كَ at the end, which makes it a complete demonstrative pronoun meaning 'that'
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'
Connected the root letters and added kasra to create a noun meaning the system or way that is followed
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject being described by the word ٱلْقَيِّمَةِ (the correct/straight) in this ayah
Basic meaning of standing upright or being established firmly
The doubled ي (with shadda) makes the meaning stronger, like 'very upright'
Added ال to specify this particular upright religion
Added kasra at the end because it's describing the word دِينُ (religion) in this ayah

This is a special word that emphasizes the truth of what follows. In this ayah, it emphasizes that those who disbelieved (كَفَرُوا۟) will definitely be in the fire of Hell. It's like adding 'truly' or 'certainly' at the start of a sentence.
This is the basic form that points to someone or something being described
Adding ٱلّ makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'

steps.m: those who (referring to multiple people who كَفَرُوا۟)
steps.n: Adding ن at the end makes it refer to multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to multiple people who disbelieved (كَفَرُوا۟)
Added fathas to create basic past tense verb form
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action. This word describes what the people mentioned in إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ (indeed those who) did.

This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or what group it belongs to. Here it connects 'those who disbelieved' (كَفَرُوا) with 'the people' (أَهْلِ) to show which people are being talked about.
Connected the root letters with fatha and sukun to make the noun 'people/family'. This word is part of the phrase أهل الكتاب (people of the book).
Added kasra at the end to show this word owns or belongs to something (in this case, الكِتٰبِ - the book)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he wrote' into 'something written' by using a common pattern for making nouns from action words
Added the definite article ال to specify a particular book
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by أَهْلِ (people) in the phrase 'people of the book'

steps.m: to join or associate something with something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show who does the action

steps.m: multiple people who join/associate partners (with Allah)
steps.n: Added ين to show we're talking about many people

steps.m: the people who join/associate partners (with Allah)
steps.n: Added ال to point to specific polytheists being discussed

steps.m: and the polytheists (joined with أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتٰبِ - People of the Book)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this word with 'People of the Book' mentioned before it, and added َ at the end to show it's part of the same grammatical group
Basic particle showing location or position inside something
Added ى at the end to complete the particle. This word shows where something is located - in this case, it shows the disbelievers are in the fire of Hell.
Basic root letters for 'light' with vowels added to make it pronounceable
The weak letter و changes to ا, and tanween is added to make it indefinite
The word 'fire' gets a kasra (small diagonal line) under the last letter because it's part of the phrase 'fire of Hell'
This is the proper name for Hell in Arabic. The shadda (double letter mark) on ن shows emphasis.

steps.m: Hell (as the object after فِى نَارِ - in the fire of)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this is the object after 'in the fire of' in the ayah

steps.m: to stay forever, to be eternal

steps.n: Active participle form showing someone/something that stays forever. This word describes the disbelievers (الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا) mentioned earlier in the verse.
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) called an overbar, which is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning
Added ين at the end to make it plural, showing it refers to multiple people who remain forever

steps.m: those who remain forever (as the object)
steps.n: Added َ (fatha) at the end because this word is the object/receiver of an action (like when someone does something to 'them')
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment

steps.m: in it (referring to نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ - the Fire of Hell)
steps.n: Added the feminine pronoun هَا to show what is being referred to (the Fire)

steps.m: in it (referring to نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ - the Fire of Hell)
steps.n: The alif is elongated for emphasis in recitation, but doesn't change the meaning
Basic demonstrative word for pointing to things
The long alif (ٰٓ) shows distance - that we're pointing to something far away

steps.m: those (pointing to the disbelievers and polytheists mentioned before)
steps.n: Adding ئِكَ makes it specifically point to something mentioned before. In this ayah, it refers back to the disbelievers and polytheists who were just mentioned.
This is a pronoun used to refer back to the disbelievers (الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا) mentioned earlier in the verse. It shows we're talking about multiple people (all male or a mixed group).
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: he/it was evil (as a permanent quality)
steps.n: Changed the middle vowel to damma to show this is a permanent trait

steps.m: to create from nothing, to bring something new into existence

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to create' to noun meaning 'something that was created'
Adds ة to make it feminine and mean 'a creation' instead of just 'related to creation'

steps.m: of creation (referring to all created beings)
steps.n: The ِ shows this word comes after شَرُّ (worst) in the phrase 'worst of creation'
Makes the noun definite to refer to all creation in general
This is the basic conditional particle that shows something depends on something else

steps.m: indeed/verily (emphasizes the truth of what follows)
steps.n: Adding the shadda changes this from a conditional word to one that emphasizes the truth of what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes the truth about those who believe and do good deeds.
This is the basic form of the word used to connect descriptions to things or people, like saying 'the one who' in English
Adding ٱلّ makes it more specific, like adding 'the' in English
Adding ن at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one. This word connects to ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed) in this ayah, describing who did the believing.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters and show past tense meaning
Changed first letter to hamza and added alif, making the meaning stronger
Added وا to show many people did this action. This word connects to وَعَمِلُوا۟ (and they did) in the ayah.

steps.m: to work, to act, to do deeds

steps.n: Adding vowels makes it a past tense verb
The وا ending changes 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural) in the past tense, showing multiple people did the action
Added و at beginning to connect this word to the previous word ءَامَنُوا۟, showing these actions happened together - they believed AND did good deeds
Changes from verb 'to be good' to noun meaning 'one who is good'
Adding ات at the end makes it mean 'many righteous deeds' instead of 'one righteous thing'
Adding ال makes it specific/definite, like saying 'the' in English
The ِ at the end shows this word is connected to the word before it
The ٱلْ means 'the' and makes it specific. The ِ at the end shows this word is connected to the verb 'did/performed' that comes before it
This is the base form meaning 'those'
The alif with line (ٰ) shows distance - pointing to something far away
Adding ئِ makes it more specific, like saying 'those specific ones'

steps.m: those ones there (far away) (subject of هُمْ)
steps.n: Adding كَ completes the word. This word is the subject that هُمْ (they) refers back to in this ayah.

This is a personal pronoun meaning 'they' or 'them' when talking about a group of men or mixed group. In this ayah, it refers back to 'those who believed and did good deeds' (ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟) and is the subject of خَيْرُ (best).
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it a noun describing quality
The ُ at the end shows this is describing what هُمْ (they) are. Together هُمْ خَيْرُ means 'they are best'.

steps.m: to create or bring into existence

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to create' to adjective meaning 'free/innocent'
Adds feminine marker to make it refer to a female
Adds 'the' to make it definite

steps.m: of the created beings (خَيْرُ - best of)
steps.n: Changed from 'innocent one' to 'creation/creatures' - different word with similar spelling. Gets kasra ending because it comes after خَيْرُ (best of)

steps.m: to give what is due (good or bad)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form past tense verb
Changed from action to the thing resulting from the action
Changed to show this is the reward itself (as the main doer) rather than just any reward
Added هُمْ to show the reward belongs to them

steps.m: to be present, to be near
steps.n: This word is used to show location or possession, like saying something is 'at' or 'with' someone
Adding the kasra and sukun creates a preposition showing location or possession. In this ayah, it shows where the gardens (جَنَّاتُ) are located - with/near their Lord.
Adding the fatha at the end helps connect this word to what comes after it in the sentence

steps.m: Lord (the one who cares for and develops everything)
steps.n: The doubled ب (with shadda) shows this is someone who completely and perfectly does this caring and developing

steps.m: their Lord (referring to the believers mentioned earlier)
steps.n: The هِمْ shows this Lord belongs to or is connected to them (the believers). This word is part of the phrase 'their reward with their Lord' (جَزَآؤُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ)
Connected the root letters and added the shadda to show emphasis
Adding ة changes it from a verb into a noun meaning the place where things are hidden/covered by plants
Transforms singular garden into plural form

steps.m: gardens (as the subject, referring to جَزَآؤُهُمْ/their reward)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is the subject, connecting to 'their reward' mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: eternal dwelling/paradise (name of a special garden)
steps.n: This is the basic form of the word عدن which is a proper noun referring to a special type of paradise or garden in heaven. The word suggests permanence and eternal stay.
Added the tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this word describes the gardens (جَنَّاتُ) that come before it. It's like saying 'gardens of paradise' in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added تَ to show this is happening in the present tense
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts. Here it shows the rivers flow from beneath the gardens.
This word describes location below something else
Adding vowel marks to make the word readable and give it its proper noun form
Changed final vowel to kasra to show this connects to what comes after it
Added هَا to show this refers to the gardens (جَنَّاتُ) mentioned earlier in the verse
Root meaning 'flow' becomes the noun 'river' by adding vowels. Like turning 'flow' into 'river' in English.
Changed to plural form by adding أ at the start and ا after ه
Added ال to make it specific
Changed the 'a' sound to a longer 'aa' sound and added 'u' at the end to show 'the rivers' is doing the flowing
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show who does the action
Added ين to show it's talking about many people, and changed the first alif to a special long alif (ٰ)
Basic preposition showing location or containment
Added feminine pronoun هَا to show what is being referred to. The هَا refers back to جَنَّاتُ (gardens) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Changed the alif to an extended alif (آ) for pronunciation emphasis at the end of the word. This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make a noun meaning endless time

steps.m: forever (describing how long they will stay)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to make it describe how long the staying will last, connecting to خٰلِدِينَ (dwelling) in the ayah
Added vowels to show past tense and the و changes to ي at the end. This word describes how Allah felt about them.
This is already in the correct form - no changes needed
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes it, and the ُ shows it's the subject of رَّضِىَ (is pleased with) in this ayah.
Basic preposition

steps.m: about them (connected to رَضِىَ - he was pleased)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this is about 'them'. This word connects to رَضِىَ (he was pleased) to say 'he was pleased with them'.
Connected the root letters and added vowels. The weak letter و changes to ي in past tense.
Changes to plural masculine past tense (they). The ي is dropped when وا is added.

steps.m: and they became pleased (with عَنْهُ/with Him)
steps.n: Adds و at beginning to mean 'and'. This word connects to عَنْهُ (with Him) in the next part of the ayah.
Basic preposition

steps.m: about him (connected to رَضِىَ - He was pleased)
steps.n: Added هُ (him) to show who the action is about. This connects to the verb رَضِىَ (He was pleased) in the ayah, showing Allah was pleased about him.
Basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
Adding لِ changes it from pointing to something near to pointing to something far

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) (refers to جَزَآؤُهُمْ/their reward)
steps.n: Adding كَ makes it more specific and emphatic. In this ayah, it's pointing back to their reward (جَزَآؤُهُمْ) mentioned earlier.
Basic preposition showing purpose or direction. Here it connects to the verb خَشِىَ (feared) to show who gets the reward.
Added مَن which means 'whoever' - it introduces the person who will get the reward mentioned before

steps.m: he feared (subject of رَبَّهُۥ - his Lord)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show it's a past tense verb. This word shows who did the action of fearing their Lord.

steps.m: Lord (one who nurtures and cares completely)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures and cares completely and perfectly
Adding هُۥ at the end shows this belongs to 'him'. This word is what is being feared in the phrase 'feared his Lord'
